meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Solve 'No Project Ideas' for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap with our SaaS starter kit. A guide for students to build real-world apps using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from over-analyzing features rather than solving friction. By using a SaaS starter kit, you bypass boilerplate, allowing you to deploy a functional MVP in hours. Focus on solving one specific workflow for a niche audience to build a high-value portfolio asset immediately.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. You have the technical skills, the documentation, and the drive, yet the 'perfect idea' remains elusive. This is the valley of death for every aspiring software engineer—the paralyzing gap between knowing how to code and knowing what to build.

But here is the secret: you don't need a revolutionary idea; you need a repeatable process. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you strip away the friction of authentication, database schema design, and deployment. You move from 'thinking' to 'shipping,' transforming the blank screen into a production-ready application that proves your worth to future employers.

steps.title: Define the Niche Problem
steps.description: Identify a small, boring workflow within a specific industry. Focus on where manual data entry or spreadsheet chaos exists.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a product strategist. Generate 5 micro-SaaS ideas for freelance graphic designers that solve specific data-entry friction points. Provide a target user persona for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate-product-brief.md' based on the top selected niche.

steps.title: Bootstrap with Starter Kit
steps.description: Clone your SaaS starter kit to establish the foundational architecture (Auth, Stripe, Database).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the boilerplate to include Supabase auth and Stripe checkout. Ensure all environment variables are mapped to a secure .env.example file.

steps.title: Build the Core Value Loop
steps.description: Implement the single feature that solves the user's main pain point. Ignore all 'nice-to-haves' for now.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service layer function in TypeScript that processes user input and returns a structured analysis, handling potential edge cases for empty inputs.

steps.title: Integrate AI Intelligence
steps.description: Inject LLM capabilities to automate the manual task identified in step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API route that uses Claude's SDK to parse user-uploaded CSV files and categorize them into professional tags.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Push your application to a live URL to validate your project in the real world.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment plan for Vercel, including build commands and environment variable configuration settings.

pro_tips: Focus on 'pain-killer' SaaS, not 'vitamin' SaaS; solve a problem people are currently paying to fix manually.
Ship the MVP in 48 hours; if it takes longer, you are over-engineering the feature set.
Use your commit history as a documentation log; it proves your process to recruiters more than the final code.
Automate your testing early; a bug-free prototype is worth ten buggy 'feature-rich' apps.
Collect user feedback from the first 5 people who use your app; their complaints are your next iteration roadmap.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, share it on LinkedIn or X with a 'build in public' post. Gather feedback, refine the UI, and start your second project using the same kit.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder. The difference between a candidate who gets hired and one who waits is a portfolio of shipped, functional code. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the one that proves you can solve problems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
